summary: Remote MCP transports remain blocked until OAuth protected-resource metadata, resource indicators, per-user scoping, rate limits, and audit controls are implemented and verified.

required_controls.title: OAuth protected resource metadata
required_controls.requirement: A remote MCP endpoint must behave as an OAuth protected resource and publish RFC 9728 protected resource metadata with authorization server discovery.
required_controls.verification: Unauthenticated HTTP requests return a 401 WWW-Authenticate header with resource_metadata, and the well-known protected-resource metadata endpoint returns authorization_servers.

required_controls.title: Resource indicators and token audience
required_controls.requirement: MCP clients must request access tokens with the remote MCP resource indicator and the server must reject tokens whose audience/resource does not match this deployment.
required_controls.verification: Token validation tests reject valid issuer tokens without the configured MCP resource audience and accept only tokens containing the expected resource indicator.

required_controls.title: Per-user tool scoping
required_controls.requirement: Every remote MCP call must resolve an authenticated OJTFlow user and apply the same owner, role, tool permission, write gate, and review gate boundaries used by the API Assistant.
required_controls.verification: Remote MCP contract tests prove user A cannot list, inspect, mutate, or retrieve user B workflows, sessions, artifacts, reviews, judgments, or audit rows.

required_controls.title: Remote rate limits and budgets
required_controls.requirement: Remote MCP calls must enforce user and tenant budgets for chat, retrieval, upload/extraction, workflow writes, reindexing, and external provider calls.
required_controls.verification: Rate-limit tests cover read tools, write tools, high-cost OCR/LLM routes, and reindex/export operations with structured retry-after errors.

required_controls.title: Audit correlation
required_controls.requirement: Remote MCP calls must write generic audit records with user, request, client, session, tool, workflow, workflow-event refs, input hash, output hash, and redacted metadata.
required_controls.verification: Local Assistant and MCP tool calls are covered by F116 audit records; remote deployment must add client ID, issuer, token subject, resource indicator, and remote transport metadata before exposure.

required_controls.title: Tool manifest review
required_controls.requirement: Remote tool exposure must be generated from the backend allowlist and reviewed for permission scope, PHI sensitivity, external-network use, destructive behavior, and approval requirement.
required_controls.verification: Catalog validation fails if a remote-exposed tool lacks permission tags, risk level, approval metadata, or allowed-agent scope.
